title: Leblon

infobox.value: (Portuguese pronunciation: [[leˈblõ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Portuguese "Help:IPA/Portuguese")) is a neighborhood of [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), Brazil. It is also the name of the local beach. The neighborhood is located in the [South Zone](/wiki/South_Zone_(Rio_de_Janeiro) "South Zone (Rio de Janeiro)") of the city, between [Lagoa Rodrigo de Freitas](/wiki/Lagoa_Rodrigo_de_Freitas "Lagoa Rodrigo de Freitas"), Morro Dois Irmãos and the Jardim de Alah channel, bordering the [Gávea](/wiki/G%C3%A1vea,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Gávea, Rio de Janeiro"), [Ipanema](/wiki/Ipanema,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Ipanema, Rio de Janeiro"), [Lagoa](/wiki/Lagoa,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Lagoa, Rio de Janeiro"), and [Vidigal](/wiki/Vidigal,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Vidigal, Rio de Janeiro") neighborhoods. It is regarded as a very affluent area.

was a quilombo (settlement of escaped African slaves) that existed at the end of the 19th century in the present-day region of [Clube Campestre da Guanabara](/w/index.php?title=Clube_Campestre_da_Guanabara&action=edit&redlink=1 "Clube Campestre da Guanabara (page does not exist)") and surroundings from what is now Rua Timothy Da Costa to Morro Dois Irmãos (in English "Two Brothers Hill") in [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro").[[3]](#cite_note-3)
["RA VI - Lagoa"](http://portalgeo.rio.rj.gov.br/bairroscariocas/mostra_temas_ra.php?ra=Lagoa&area=06&tipo=click) (in Portuguese). Prefeitura da Cidade do Rio de Janeiro. Retrieved 25 September 2014.

**Leblon** (Portuguese pronunciation: [[leˈblõ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Portuguese "Help:IPA/Portuguese")) is a neighborhood of [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), Brazil. It is also the name of the local beach. The neighborhood is located in the [South Zone](/wiki/South_Zone_(Rio_de_Janeiro) "South Zone (Rio de Janeiro)") of the city, between [Lagoa Rodrigo de Freitas](/wiki/Lagoa_Rodrigo_de_Freitas "Lagoa Rodrigo de Freitas"), Morro Dois Irmãos and the Jardim de Alah channel, bordering the [Gávea](/wiki/G%C3%A1vea,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Gávea, Rio de Janeiro"), [Ipanema](/wiki/Ipanema,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Ipanema, Rio de Janeiro"), [Lagoa](/wiki/Lagoa,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Lagoa, Rio de Janeiro"), and [Vidigal](/wiki/Vidigal,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Vidigal, Rio de Janeiro") neighborhoods. It is regarded as a very affluent area.

Leblon began as a [quilombo](/wiki/Quilombo "Quilombo") of escaped slaves created by a Portuguese abolitionist landowner.

## Etymology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Leblon&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Etymology")]

The neighborhood is named for Carlos Leblon, a [whaling](/wiki/Whaling "Whaling") empresario [of French origin](/wiki/French_Brazilian "French Brazilian") who possessed a [chácara](/wiki/Chacra "Chacra") in the region since 1845. Before the area was urbanized it was known as *Campo do Leblon* (Leblon's Field).

## Early history

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Leblon&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Early history")]

The **Quilombo of Leblon** was a quilombo (settlement of escaped African slaves) that existed at the end of the 19th century in the present-day region of [Clube Campestre da Guanabara](/w/index.php?title=Clube_Campestre_da_Guanabara&action=edit&redlink=1 "Clube Campestre da Guanabara (page does not exist)") and surroundings from what is now Rua Timothy Da Costa to Morro Dois Irmãos (in English "Two Brothers Hill") in [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The creator of the quilombo was the Portuguese [José de Seixas Magalhães](/w/index.php?title=Jos%C3%A9_de_Seixas_Magalh%C3%A3es&action=edit&redlink=1 "José de Seixas Magalhães (page does not exist)"),[[4]](#cite_note-veja-4) who dedicated himself to the manufacture and trade of suitcases.[[5]](#cite_note-5) and pod bags on Rua Gonçalves Dias, at the center of the city. His bags were made in a factory with [steam engine](/wiki/Steam_engine "Steam engine"). In addition to the luggage factory, Seixas also owned a [farm](/wiki/Farm "Farm") in Leblon where he cultivated flowers with the help of [slaves](/wiki/Slavery_in_Brazil "Slavery in Brazil") fugitives. Seixas hid the fugitives in the Leblon farm with the help of the main [abolitionists](/wiki/Abolitionism "Abolitionism") from the capital of [Empire](/wiki/Empire_of_Brazil "Empire of Brazil"), many of them members of [Abolitionist Confederation](/wiki/Brazilian_Abolitionist_Confederation "Brazilian Abolitionist Confederation"). The Seixas flower farm was known as the "quilombo Leblon", a name that referred to the former owner of the region, the Frenchman Carlos Leblon. It was in the Quilombo do Leblon that Seixas cultivated his famous [camellias](/wiki/Camellia "Camellia"), which were the symbol of the abolitionist movement.

The Quilombo do Leblon had the protection of [Princess Isabel](/wiki/Isabel,_Princess_Imperial_of_Brazil "Isabel, Princess Imperial of Brazil"). As a token of gratitude, Seixas regularly supplied camellias to [Isabel Palace](/wiki/Guanabara_Palace "Guanabara Palace"), the princess’s residence in Laranjeiras (today, the seat of the government of the [State of Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro_(state) "Rio de Janeiro (state)")). The camellias of Seixas adorned the Princess’s work table and her private [chapel](/wiki/Chapel "Chapel"), where she made her prayers. In addition to the camellias, Seixas also offered the golden penalty to the Princess Regent who, later, on 13 May 1888, would be used to sign the [Golden Law](/wiki/Golden_Law "Golden Law"). The quilombo gave rise to the current name of the neighborhood of Leblon.

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Leblon&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Economy")]

The average nominal monthly income of people aged 10 or over (with income) in Leblon is R$6,844.63 (according to the 2010 [census](/wiki/Census "Census") by the [Brazilian Institute of Geography and Statistics](/wiki/Brazilian_Institute_of_Geography_and_Statistics "Brazilian Institute of Geography and Statistics")). Leblon is one of the most sought-after neighborhoods in the city and the one that increases in value each year. It has the most expensive square meter in the country, costing [R$](/wiki/Brazilian_real "Brazilian real")18,332. In areas close to the beach, however, this value can reach R$20,000.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Characteristics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Leblon&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Characteristics")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/23/Praia_do_Leblon_2017.jpg/250px-Praia_do_Leblon_2017.jpg)](/wiki/File:Praia_do_Leblon_2017.jpg)

Leblon Beach.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f8/Leblon_3_by_Diego_Baravelli.jpg/250px-Leblon_3_by_Diego_Baravelli.jpg)](/wiki/File:Leblon_3_by_Diego_Baravelli.jpg)

Residential buildings. Leblon has the most expensive price per residential square meter in Latin America.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1a/Mapa_de_Leblon.jpg/330px-Mapa_de_Leblon.jpg)](/wiki/File:Mapa_de_Leblon.jpg)

Map of Leblon.

It is located west of [Ipanema](/wiki/Ipanema "Ipanema"). In the north, it is bordered by [Gávea](/wiki/G%C3%A1vea,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Gávea, Rio de Janeiro") and, in the west, by a towering hill called Dois Irmãos, which translates as "two brothers", because of its split peak.

## Leblon in popular culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Leblon&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Leblon in popular culture")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/db/Leblonevidigal.jpg/250px-Leblonevidigal.jpg)](/wiki/File:Leblonevidigal.jpg)

Leblon beach and favela Vidigal.

Leblon is known for being a very wealthy and cosmopolitan neighborhood, with a lively nightlife across its bars, restaurants and nightclubs. Leblon has been either referenced or depicted in the following media:

In television:

* *[Viver a Vida](/wiki/Viver_a_Vida "Viver a Vida")*, a [telenovela](/wiki/Telenovela "Telenovela") produced by [Rede Globo](/wiki/Rede_Globo "Rede Globo"), aired from September 2009 to May 2010;
* *[Páginas da Vida](/wiki/P%C3%A1ginas_da_Vida_(Brazilian_TV_series) "Páginas da Vida (Brazilian TV series)")*, a telenovela produced by Rede Globo, aired from July 2006 to March 2007;
* *[Mulheres Apaixonadas](/wiki/Mulheres_Apaixonadas "Mulheres Apaixonadas")*, a telenovela produced by Rede Globo, aired from February to October 2003;
* *[Laços de Familia](/wiki/La%C3%A7os_de_Familia "Laços de Familia")*, a telenovela produced by Rede Globo, aired from June 2000 to February 2001;
* *[Por Amor](/wiki/Por_Amor_(1997_telenovela) "Por Amor (1997 telenovela)")*, a telenovela produced by Rede Globo, aired from October 1997 to May 1998;
* *[História de Amor](/wiki/Hist%C3%B3ria_de_Amor "História de Amor")*, a telenovela produced by Rede Globo, aired from July 1995 to March 1996.

In music:
Leblon has been the subject of many songs, such as

* "Falso Leblon", "Choque de Ordem", "Haiti", "O namorado" and "O quereres" by [Caetano Veloso](/wiki/Caetano_Veloso "Caetano Veloso");
* "Óculos" by [Paralamas do Sucesso](/wiki/Paralamas_do_Sucesso "Paralamas do Sucesso");
* "Inverno" by [Adriana Calcanhotto](/wiki/Adriana_Calcanhotto "Adriana Calcanhotto");
* "Sexo, amor, traição" by [Luciana Mello](/wiki/Luciana_Mello "Luciana Mello");
* "Andar, andar" and "Tesoura do desejo" by [Alceu Valença](/wiki/Alceu_Valen%C3%A7a "Alceu Valença");
* "Aquilo bom (garotas do Leblon)" by [Elba Ramalho](/wiki/Elba_Ramalho "Elba Ramalho");
* "Daqui pro Méier" by [Ed Motta](/wiki/Ed_Motta "Ed Motta");
* "Balanço Zona Sul" by [Tito Madi](/wiki/Tito_Madi "Tito Madi");
* "Completamente Blue" by [Cazuza](/wiki/Cazuza "Cazuza");
* "Virgem" by [Marina Lima](/wiki/Marina_Lima "Marina Lima");
* "A vida tem dessas coisas" by [Ritchie](/wiki/Ritchie_(vocalist) "Ritchie (vocalist)")
* "Tardes no Leblon" by Alberto Rosenblit.

In cinema
in the 22 de march de 2011 the premiere internacional in the rio (2011) he was in [Leblon, Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Leblon,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Leblon, Rio de Janeiro").
